verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is a secreted trypsin-like serine protease without a transmembrane domain, normally produced by prostate epithelial cells and secreted into prostatic fluid. However, recent studies demonstrate that KLK2 is stably expressed on the cell surface in prostate cancer cells and patient-derived tumors, where it is targetable by bispecific T-cell engagers and other cell-surface-directed therapeutics. This cancer-cell-restricted surface display represents oncogenic transformation-induced ectodomain presentation of a nominally secreted protein, fitting the cell_state_induced contextual mechanism.
